context: Tests whether the agent computes per-category ratios, cost-per-DAU, and produces the right slack/page alerts per the documented thresholds, with comments on per-environment isolation.

checklist.name: Computes totals
checklist.description: Both current.total and previous.total in the return are the sum of all category values for that week

checklist.name: Cost-per-DAU per week
checklist.description: Both current.costPerDau and previous.costPerDau equal total / dau for that week

checklist.name: Per-category ratios
checklist.description: categoryRatios contains an entry for every category in the input, each equal to current/previous for that category

checklist.name: dominantCategoryCurrent is argmax
checklist.description: dominantCategoryCurrent is the key in current.categories whose value is the maximum

checklist.name: Page-level threshold for category
checklist.description: A category whose ratio is strictly greater than categoryPageMultiplier produces an alert entry { level: 'page', category, ratio }

checklist.name: Slack-level threshold for category
checklist.description: A category whose ratio is strictly greater than categorySlackMultiplier but not greater than categoryPageMultiplier produces an alert entry { level: 'slack', category, ratio }

checklist.name: No double-alert
checklist.description: When a category exceeds the page multiplier, the result contains exactly one alert entry for that category (the 'page' entry), not both page and slack

checklist.name: Total can also alert
checklist.description: Same threshold logic applies to total with totalSlackMultiplier/totalPageMultiplier, pushed with category: 'total'

checklist.name: Comment on per-keyset attribution
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly states the review is per-keyset (per-environment) and that mixing environments distorts attribution

checklist.name: Comment on cost-per-DAU normalization
checklist.description: Top-of-file comment explicitly says cost-per-DAU normalization is what reveals per-user efficiency changes, and notes that flat cost-per-DAU with rising DAU is usually fine
